title: Writing a critical review
explanation:
Writing a critical review

A critical review evaluates a book, film or article with balanced judgement and precise language.

questions.text: [*Although the plot is predictable, the performances are compelling.|Plot predictable although performances compelling.|Although performances plot predictable.]
[*The director juxtaposes wealth and poverty to expose social tension.|Director juxtaposes wealth poverty social tension.|Juxtaposes director wealth poverty.]
[*On balance, the novel succeeds as a character study rather than as thriller.|Balance novel succeeds character study thriller.|Novel balance succeeds study.]
[*Readers seeking subtle moral ambiguity will not be disappointed.|Readers seeking moral ambiguity disappointed not.|Seeking readers ambiguity moral.]

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: A critical review should combine summary and ____.  | *evaluation | recipe | ticket
Use precise adjectives such as compelling, uneven and ____.  | *thought-provoking | tasty | noisy
Avoid vague praise like 'very nice' or 'pretty ____. ' | *good | loud | fast
Support judgements with brief ____.  | *examples | jokes | titles only
[*The pacing falters in the second half.|Pacing falters second half the.|Second half pacing falters.]
[*The author's prose is economical yet evocative.|Author prose economical evocative yet.|Prose author economical.]
[*Ultimately, the film raises more questions than it answers.|Ultimately film raises questions answers than.|Film ultimately questions raises.]
[*I would recommend it to viewers who enjoy slow, reflective drama.|Recommend viewers enjoy slow reflective drama.|Viewers recommend enjoy drama.]
